purpose: Sweep profit_lock_trail_pct on 3 J winner days to find optimal trail
recommendation: Best trail_pct=0.20 still misses OP-16 (edge_capture=-358 < 771). Root cause may require exit redesign beyond chandelier trail widening (e.g., underlying-price-based runner stop).

root_cause_diagnosis.finding: Trail-pct is NOT the bottleneck. All 8 trail variants produce identical results.
root_cause_diagnosis.explanation: The premium stop (-8%) triggers BEFORE the chandelier ever arms (+5% threshold). Engine enters at midday (11:25-13:45 ET) during sustained trends when J entered at open (09:35-10:30 ET). max_fav == entry_premium on most trades -- premium immediately adverse after entry. SPY bounces from intraday low right after engine entry, hitting -8% stop before trend resumes.

per_day_detail.2026-04-29: Engine entry 13:45 ET (.07 ATM), immediately adverse, max_fav=entry. J entered ~10:00 AM at first level rejection.
per_day_detail.2026-05-01: Two engine entries: 12:20 + 13:55 ET, both immediately adverse. J entered in morning.
per_day_detail.2026-05-04: Engine entry 11:25 ET (.79), barely favorable (+4.5%), not enough to arm chandelier at +5%. J entered ~10:00 AM.

root_cause_diagnosis.correct_diagnosis: ENTRY TIMING, not exit mechanics. Engine requires ribbon confirmation (3+ bars of lower closes) before entry, missing the morning rejection window J uses.
root_cause_diagnosis.corrected_research_direction: v15.3 (live-price trigger at PML/PMH/Carry ★★+ levels in 09:35-09:45 ET window) IS the correct fix. NOT chandelier widening. The v15.3 trigger would have caught the morning level rejections on all 3 winner days. See: strategy/candidates/2026-05-17-live-price-first-bar-trigger.md
root_cause_diagnosis.op16_path: v15.3 ratification scorecard currently fails OP-16 gate (4/7 pass) because the BASELINE (v15.1) already fails OP-16. The scorecard needs to be re-evaluated on: does v15.3 CAPTURE the morning rejections on 4/29+5/01+5/04? If yes, edge_capture from winner days may cross OP-16 floor even if loser days unchanged.
